title: LandCoverNet Global Land-Cover Training Dataset

data_urls.access_notes: Regional Source Cooperative record with dataset documentation and object paths.
Use for benchmark motivation and dataset construction details.

instructions: Use Radiant MLHub or Source Cooperative records rather than scraping imagery from arbitrary mirrors.
Record region/continent, chip ID, coordinates, sensor, year, spectral bands, label map, and split.
Start with one continent or region to test SPECTRA axes before expanding globally.

scale.download_size: 8,941 chips of 256 x 256 pixels across 300 geographically diverse tiles, with matched multi-sensor imagery

recommended_axes.name: geographic_distance
recommended_axes.definition: Geodesic, tile, region, or continent distance from training chips.

recommended_axes.name: sensor_support
recommended_axes.definition: Similarity and overlap across Sentinel-1, Sentinel-2, and Landsat-8 observations.

recommended_axes.name: land_cover_taxonomy_support
recommended_axes.definition: Class hierarchy and label-prevalence support for land-cover categories.

recommended_axes.name: biome_or_environmental_support
recommended_axes.definition: Climate, biome, vegetation, or environmental-distance support if auxiliary metadata are joined.

useful_for: A concrete, scoped geospatial generalization dataset beyond WILDS PovertyMap.
Testing whether land-cover models degrade with geography, sensor, and ecological novelty.
Constructing prospective spatial splits with explicit near-tile leakage checks.
leakage_risks: Adjacent chips from the same tile can leak spatial context.
Multi-sensor views of the same location can leak if treated as independent samples.
Label-source artifacts and regional class prevalence can masquerade as model generalization.

paperclip_provenance.notes: Paperclip identified LandCoverNet and related global land-cover resources; official NASA/Radiant/Source Cooperative pages confirm portable access and scale.
